examples.title: Basic BST - Node, Insert, and Search
examples.description: Demonstrates fundamental BST operations including node definition, insert, and search functions
examples.explanation: 1. Node class stores key and left/right child pointers. 2. BST class manages the tree with root pointer. 3. Insert follows BST property: left subtree values < node value, right subtree values > node value. 4. Search compares target with current node and traverses left or right accordingly. 5. Time complexity is O(h) where h is height - O(log n) for balanced tree, O(n) worst case.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) for recursion stack, O(1) auxiliary

examples.title: BST Delete Operation
examples.description: Implements the delete operation handling all three cases: leaf node, node with one child, and node with two children
examples.explanation: 1. Delete operation handles three cases: Case 1 - leaf node (remove directly), Case 2 - node with one child (replace with child), Case 3 - node with two children (find inorder successor or predecessor). 2. Inorder successor (smallest value in right subtree) replaces deleted node's value. 3. Then recursively delete the successor from right subtree. 4. The _delete_recursive function returns the updated subtree root.

examples.title: Advanced BST - Complete Operations with Validation
examples.description: Advanced implementation including tree validation, height calculation, floor/ceiling operations, and range queries
examples.explanation: 1. Height calculation uses recursion, returning -1 for empty tree. 2. BST validation checks that each node falls within min/max bounds from its ancestors. 3. Floor returns largest value ≤ key using iterative approach. 4. Ceiling returns smallest value ≥ key. 5. Range query performs inorder traversal with bounds checking for efficient range searches. 6. All operations maintain O(log n) average time for balanced trees.
examples.timeComplexity: O(log n) average, O(n) worst for all operations
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) for recursion, O(1) for iterative operations
